title: Sukju Empanadas con Soffritto y Semillas de Mungo
description: Sour and flaky Uruguayan empanadas filled with mung-bean-sprout-sukju and soffritto. Perfect for a kid-friendly snack or lunch.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, lard, and salt. Mix until a dough forms.
Gradually add water to the dough and knead for 10 minutes until smooth.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
In a small pan, sauté the onion and garlic over medium heat until softened.
Add the mung-bean sprouts, sukju, and soffritto base to the pan and stir to combine.
Place a tablespoon of the filling mixture onto one half of each dough circle.
Fold the other half of the dough circle over the filling and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and fold in the banana leaves to create a package.
Cook the empanadas in a steamer basket over boiling water for 15 minutes.
